title: Dense Yeasty Thai Celeriac Nam Prik
description: A rich, satisfying post-workout Central Thai dish infused with the deep flavors of celeriac and warm spices.
instructions: Combine celeriac and yeast in a mixing bowl, adding 1 cup of lukewarm water to activate the yeast.
Knead the mixture for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl and let it ferment in a warm place for 2 hours.
Divide the dough into 4 equal portions and shape each into a small disk.
Heat coconut oil in a pan over medium heat, then add grated ginger and sauté for 2 minutes.
Add fish sauce, palm sugar, and salt to the pan, stirring to combine.
Place the celeriac disks on the preheated salt block and sear for 2-3 minutes on each side.
Transfer the cooked celeriac to a serving platter and drizzle with infused oil, then garnish with cilantro.
Slice chili peppers and place on top of the celeriac disks.
